text: ‘Trust him if thou wilt, true thou shalt find him’; literally, ‘those who trust in him shall understand truth’. The word ‘truth’ in the Old Testament refers, as a rule, either to human loyalty or to divine fidelity.
‘His chosen servants’; the Greek text gives, ‘His holy ones; who shall find deliverance, if not his chosen servants?’
Or perhaps, ‘The scheming of the wicked shall recoil on them in punishment’.
Cf. Deut. 23.1; Is. 56.3. ‘He too with rare gifts shall be faithfully rewarded’; literally, ‘He too shall be given a rare gift of fidelity’.
Some think that adultery, here as often in the Old Testament, is used by a metaphor for the worship of false gods.
